cover.title: cheaper is the wrong ai business case
cover.sub: 'ship the same software with fewer people' is a mispriced goal. here's the one that isn't.

slides.title: what cheap erodes
slides.body: optimize for fewer people and you quietly trade away the standards worth keeping: review depth, current context, the senior judgment that catches the wrong call.

slides.title: one real number
slides.body: a canonical context layer cut our agents' context to ~60% fewer tokens per lookup. first-party, trovex own-run.

slides.title: the goal that isn't mispriced
slides.body: done well, ai is the team you already trust, made more capable. that compounds. cheap doesn't.

cta.title: what agents actually cost
cta.sub: the bill isn't the license. it's the context they re-read on every single run.
